genre_refs: WWII-era / WPA recruitment poster art; mid-century pulp illustration
description: A stylized, AI-generated illustration in the manner of a vintage 1940s-50s American recruitment/propaganda poster. It depicts a clean-cut, square-jawed young white man with neatly combed brown hair, wearing a light short-sleeve work shirt, gazing confidently at the viewer. Behind him, against a pale cloud-streaked sky, are industrial elements: what appears to be a rusted pickaxe/tool on the left and the yellow boom/arm of an excavator or oil-pump machinery on the right, evoking energy and construction labor. The image has the muted, slightly aged color palette and painterly shading typical of retro illustration, with a faint cream border. Across the lower portion, in large white serif (italic 'Power the' over bold 'GOLDEN AGE!'), reads the slogan 'Power the GOLDEN AGE!'. A small circular DOL/government seal sits in the bottom-right corner. The propaganda technique is heroic idealization of the American (energy/blue-collar) worker via nostalgic poster aesthetics, tying labor to national renewal ('Golden Age'). Target audience: blue-collar workers and energy-sector supporters, promoting 'American Energy Dominance.'
